title: linaria amethystea subsp. broussonetii
intro: Linaria amethystea subsp. broussonetii, also known as Broussonet's toadflax, is a plant species within the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which are believed to aid in the relief of respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis. Broussonet's toadflax is also used to treat skin conditions, including acne and eczema, due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties, as well as its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin. Herbal preparations of Linaria amethystea subsp. broussonetii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or ingested to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
